faults.name: One bridge diode is reversed
faults.why: A reversed diode breaks one conduction path and creates half-wave or clipped output.
faults.verification_test: Check each diode orientation against the bridge diagram.
faults.fix_recipe: Rotate the incorrect diode and retest the rectified output.

faults.name: Bridge negative node is not tied to circuit reference
faults.why: A floating bridge output makes the load voltage appear unstable or wrong.
faults.verification_test: Check continuity from the bridge negative output to the load return.
faults.fix_recipe: Reconnect bridge negative to the load return/reference node.

faults.name: Load resistance is too low for the source and diodes
faults.why: A heavy load can collapse the rectified output and overheat small-signal diodes.
faults.verification_test: Measure load resistance and peak output under load.
faults.fix_recipe: Use the intended load resistor and verify diode current limits.
